symptomSummary: After ~30 invocations on a revision, evolve produces new revisions addressing old problems instead of currently flagged issues.
rootCauseHypothesis: Feedback retrieval query likely lacks proper ordering (defaults to oldest-first) or filtering by current revision, causing the system to prioritize accumulated historical feedback over recent signals.
fixHypothesis: Add ORDER BY timestamp DESC or filter feedback to only recent/current-revision entries in the query that fetches feedback for evolution; potentially add a LIMIT on recent feedback only.

primaryLocation.rationale: Recently changed file containing database queries; threshold-based symptom (~30 invocations) strongly suggests a query without proper ORDER BY or revision filtering, causing it to return oldest feedback first or mix feedback across all revisions indiscriminately.

candidateLocations.rationale: Orchestrates the evolution process and likely calls the repository to fetch feedback; may be requesting feedback without specifying revision or time constraints.
Largest file in the evolution subsystem; may contain logic that processes or filters feedback incorrectly, though less likely to be the root cause than the data layer.
API endpoint for evolution; may pass incorrect parameters to the evolution service, though typically endpoints are thin wrappers.
